summary: Iteration 10 — Backend ring_number normalization + uniqueness bug fix verified. Added focused pytest module /app/backend/tests/test_ring_number_normalization.py with 16 tests covering: (a) POST /api/registered-birds rejects duplicates by exact match, lowercase, and whitespace variants; (b) fresh ring is stored normalized (uppercase, whitespace-collapsed) and lowercase/whitespace re-registration of the same ring is blocked; (c) PATCH /api/admin/registered-birds/{id} blocks case-insensitive collisions with other birds, allows same-ring different-case self-update, and normalizes stored value; (d) startup migration confirmed — all 5 seeded rings (SE100000..SE100004) present as uppercase via GET /api/admin/registered-birds. Also re-ran full existing regression suite backend_test.py (43 tests) → all green.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _normalize_ring() uses re.sub(r'\\s+', '', raw).upper() — collapses all whitespace (including internal spaces), which is consistent with what the backend enforces on write and on migration. This is intentionally strict; any pre-existing UI that stored intentionally-spaced ring numbers would now be normalized. Confirmed acceptable per problem statement ('strip whitespace').
startup migration loop (server.py lines 1662-1683) is idempotent and handles DuplicateKeyError from pre-existing collisions gracefully; safe to run on every boot.
unique index on ring_number is created before migration (line 1659) — good ordering; and DuplicateKeyError safety net exists both in POST and PATCH handlers, protecting against race conditions.
Consistent Swedish error message 'Ringnummer {ring} är redan registrerat' returned with normalized value in the message so users see what value the system compared against — good UX.
seed_data_creation: Tests create a few TEST birds via POST /api/registered-birds using randomized RB-2026-XXXXXX / TESTA-XXXXXX / TESTB-XXXXXX ring numbers, then clean them up via DELETE /api/admin/registered-birds/{id} in teardown steps.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 covered ring_number normalization only. The new test file /app/backend/tests/test_ring_number_normalization.py can be re-run standalone. Note: frontend piece (auto-uppercase input + hint text on /registrera-fagel + sonner toast on duplicate submit) was NOT tested — request scope was backend only. If UI verification is requested next, target [data-testid='ring-number-input'] on RegisterBird.jsx and verify toast shows exact backend Swedish message.
rca of the issue: Root cause: original POST/PATCH handlers compared and stored ring_number verbatim, so 'SE100000', 'se100000' and ' SE 100000 ' were treated as three distinct keys. Fix (already applied by main agent): (1) _normalize_ring() helper — collapse whitespace + uppercase; (2) apply normalize on POST + PATCH before uniqueness check; (3) unique Mongo index + DuplicateKeyError catch as race-condition safety net; (4) one-time startup migration to normalize existing docs. All four are exercised by the new tests and verified working end-to-end via the external URL.
